seo.title: Claude Tool Use Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Claude Tool Use agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Claude Tool Use face heightened compliance risks due to agentic behaviors like tool chaining and computer use, which can generate unsubstantiated product claims, inaccurate pricing, or misleading refund policies. Without guardrails, cascading tool failures or conflicting parallel calls can lead to FTC violations, PCI DSS breaches, and CCPA/GDPR non-compliance. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure Claude's outputs align with regulatory standards, preventing customer churn and enforcement actions.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; AI-generated marketing copy cannot make unverified superiority claims.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies tool use outputs against a knowledge base of substantiated claims, blocking unsubstantiated assertions before customer exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling in personalization tools must respect consent and data minimization principles.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits tool calls for PII access, ensuring Claude only processes consented data and flags over-retention.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment processing tools must not log or expose cardholder data in agent reasoning or tool outputs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans Claude's tool use chains for PCI violations, redacting sensitive payment tokens automatically.

complianceRequirements.description: Product descriptions and checkout flows generated by AI must maintain WCAG 2.1 compliance.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates AI-generated UI text and flows against accessibility rules, preventing discriminatory content.

industryRisks.name: Cascading Pricing Errors
industryRisks.description: Early tool failure in inventory check leads to incorrect pricing propagation through checkout chain.
industryRisks.example: Claude quotes $49 item at $29 due to stale cache, causing refund disputes and revenue loss.

industryRisks.name: Conflicting Refund Policy Responses
industryRisks.description: Parallel tool calls return different refund windows without resolution logic.
industryRisks.example: One tool says 30-day returns, another says 14-day, confusing customers and violating faithful representation.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Extended reasoning produces claims inconsistent with verified product data.
industryRisks.example: "Best battery life ever" generated without evidence, triggering FTC complaint.

industryRisks.name: Unintended Cart Modifications
industryRisks.description: Computer use agent clicks wrong screen elements during checkout automation.
industryRisks.example: Adds unauthorized upsell items, violating consent and causing chargebacks.

implementation.title: Verify Pricing Tool Chain
implementation.description: Wrap Claude's pricing lookup tool use with Vex to catch cascading accuracy failures.

implementation.title: Guard Refund Policy Tools
implementation.description: Ensure parallel tool calls resolve to consistent refund policy representation.
implementation.code:
import anthropic
import vex

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=[
        {"name": "get_refund_window", "description": "Return days"},
        {"name": "get_warranty_policy", "description": "Warranty terms"}
    ],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is your return policy?"}]
)

result = vex_client.verify(
    response,
    policy="ecommerce-refund-consistency",
    assertions=[
        "tool_results.refund_window == tool_results.warranty_days",
        "policy_text.contains('30 days')"
    ]
)

if result.valid:
    return response.content[0].text

implementation.title: TypeScript FTC Claim Verification
implementation.description: Verify product claims against substantiated evidence using TypeScript SDK.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Claude Tool Use in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: FashionRetailCo deployed Claude 3.5 Sonnet with tool use for their customer service chatbot, handling pricing inquiries, refund requests, and product recommendations. The agent used parallel tools to fetch inventory prices, return policies, and personalized upsells, then computer use to navigate their Shopify admin for order updates. During Black Friday, a caching bug in the inventory tool caused early pricing failures that cascaded through 15% of checkout flows, displaying $99 dresses at $49. Simultaneously, parallel refund tools returned conflicting 14-day vs 30-day windows due to regional policy differences without resolution logic. Customers posted viral complaints about bait-and-switch pricing and unclear returns, while FTC monitoring flagged unsubstantiated "fastest shipping ever" claims from the recommendation tool.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex verification around Claude's messages.create() tool responses, FashionRetailCo achieved 99.7% pricing accuracy and eliminated policy conflicts. Vex's cascading failure detection blocked 2,847 erroneous checkouts pre-customer exposure, saving $187K in refunds. FTC claim verification reduced violation rates from 8% to 0.2%, and PCI scans prevented payment token leaks during computer use automation. Customer satisfaction rose 23%, churn dropped 14%, and the compliance team gained audit-ready logs proving regulatory adherence.
